metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1253
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 2: "ADAM10 localization and activity influence spine morphology"
Figure 3: "Timing of ADAM10-dependent effect on spine morphology"

8/11/2017 Pim
- Recap: ADAM10 cleaves N-cadherin (a homophilic interactin pre and postsynaptic adhesion molecule). Mislocalization of ADAM10 with a TAT-fused competing peptide or inhibition of ADAM10 activity with TIMP-1 results in spine morphology changes and different synaptic transmission (AMPAR composition, AMPA activity) in vitro and in vivo. I think that the role of N-cadherin in this story is illustrated in a correlational rather than a experimental fashion. No effect on cell adhesion is shown.. Experimental description: Rat hippocampal cultures were used for the in vitro experiments.
Literal:
Hippocampal neuronal primary cultures were prepared from embryonic day 18-19 rat hippocampi as described previously (Piccoli et al., 2007).
Literal:
Animal treatment. Two-week-old mice (Charles River) received a single intraperitoneal injection of either TAT-Pro (3 nmol/g) or TAT-Ala peptide (3 nmol/g) diluted in sterile saline solution (Marcello et al., 2007).
